tossups.question: Tom Ruddy and Peter Sellars replaced this author’s script after this author quit Jean-Luc Godard’s King Lear, due to Godard’s suggestion that this author’s daughter play his lover. During a fight on the set of this author’s movie Maidstone, he bit off a chunk of Rip Torn’s ear. D. A. Pennebaker’s documentary Town Bloody Hall depicts this author’s debates with feminists like Germaine Greer. This author played Stanford White in Miloš Forman’s Ragtime and Harry Houdini in the second film in a series by a director who depicted his reincarnation as various cars in the film River of (*) Fundament. In this author’s film Tough Guys Don’t Dance, the camera swivels around a beach while Ryan O’Neal repeats “oh man” and “oh God.” Leon Gast interviewed George Plimpton and this author for his documentary When We Were Kings, because he had covered the film’s subject, the Rumble in the Jungle, in his book The Fight. For 10 points, name this author of The Executioner’s Song.
tossups.question_sanitized: Tom Ruddy and Peter Sellars replaced this author's script after this author quit Jean-Luc Godard's King Lear, due to Godard's suggestion that this author's daughter play his lover. During a fight on the set of this author's movie Maidstone, he bit off a chunk of Rip Torn's ear. D. A. Pennebaker's documentary Town Bloody Hall depicts this author's debates with feminists like Germaine Greer. This author played Stanford White in Milos Forman's Ragtime and Harry Houdini in the second film in a series by a director who depicted his reincarnation as various cars in the film River of (*) Fundament. In this author's film Tough Guys Don't Dance, the camera swivels around a beach while Ryan O'Neal repeats "oh man" and "oh God." Leon Gast interviewed George Plimpton and this author for his documentary When We Were Kings, because he had covered the film's subject, the Rumble in the Jungle, in his book The Fight. For 10 points, name this author of The Executioner's Song.
tossups.answer: Norman Mailer [or Nachem Malech Mailer; or Norman Kingsley Mailer] (The series is Matthew Barney’s Cremaster Cycle.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Norman Mailer [or Nachem Malech Mailer; or Norman Kingsley Mailer] (The series is Matthew Barney's Cremaster Cycle.)

bonuses.leadin: A series of linked installations, videos, art books, and films comprise this artist’s massive OTTO Trilogy, nominally focused on the life and physiology of the football player Jim Otto. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this contemporary artist known for multimedia projects like Drawing Restraint, where he painted while jumping on a trampoline or while attached to bungee cords. A five-part cycle by this artist depicts a grotesque faun lowered onto a football field via balloons.
Barney’s CREMASTER 3 was partially filmed in this New York City art museum, whose iconic white spiral ramp was designed by Frank Lloyd Wright.

In Barney’s experimental filmed opera River of Fundament, the body of Normal Mailer is represented using several of these objects. Damián Ortega’s Cosmic Thing and Chris Burden's extreme piece of body art Trans-fixed both centrally feature one of these objects.
answers_sanitized: cars [or automobiles; accept Volkswagens or Volkswagen Beetles; prompt on vehicles]
